product_overview: VidyaSetu is an AI-powered student learning platform that generates assignments, evaluates submissions with Google Gemini, tracks mastery and trends, and provides personalized remedial/enrichment recommendations. The backend is implemented with Next.js/TypeScript, Prisma + Neon, NextAuth, and Gemini AI integrations.
core_goals: Enable students to generate AI-created assignments by chapter/semester/syllabus and take tests in-app.
Auto-evaluate objective and subjective answers using a hybrid auto-grader + Gemini Pro pipeline and provide per-question feedback and overall AI feedback.
Track user progress and topic mastery over time (mastery map, trends) and surface strengths/weaknesses.
Provide AI-driven, targeted recommendations and remedial assignments for weak topics.
Expose curriculum and study materials for guided learning; allow later expansion for admin content management and leaderboards.

features.name: Assignment Management
features.description: Create, list, view, and AI-generate assignments (chapter/semester/full-syllabus). Supports quick-launch 'Generate with AI' flows and assignment detail retrieval for students to take tests.
user_flows: POST /api/assignments/generate with Authorization and body {type, chapterId/topicIds, difficulty} -> Receive 201 with {assignmentId, isAIGenerated:true} -> GET /api/assignments with Authorization -> Receive 200 list including new assignment -> GET /api/assignments/[id] with Authorization -> Receive 200 assignment details (questions, timer)

POST /api/assignments/generate with Authorization but body missing required field (e.g., no chapterId for chapter-type) -> Receive 400 validation error -> Client displays validation message and prevents generation

POST /api/assignments/generate with Authorization and valid payload -> Gemini/assignment-generator hits provider rate-limit or error -> Receive 502/500 with 'AI provider error' -> Client surfaces retry option to user

features.name: Submission and Evaluation
features.description: Submit assignment answers and retrieve AI-evaluated results including per-question feedback, percentage score, aiFeedback paragraph, and evaluatedAt timestamp. Mastery updates are applied after evaluation.
user_flows: POST /api/submissions with Authorization and body {assignmentId, answers[]} -> Receive 201 with {submissionId, status:'processing'|'completed'} -> GET /api/submissions/[id] with Authorization -> Receive 200 with evaluation {percentageScore, aiFeedback, perQuestionFeedback, evaluatedAt} -> GET /api/progress/mastery with Authorization -> Receive 200 updated mastery reflecting submission
POST /api/submissions with Authorization but payload missing answers or invalid answer format -> Receive 400 validation error -> Client shows error and prevents submission retry

POST /api/submissions with Authorization and valid payload -> Receive 201 with submissionId but evaluation-engine/Gemini Pro fails during subjective grading -> GET /api/submissions/[id] with Authorization -> Receive 500 'evaluation failed' or status:'error' -> Client instructs user to retry submission or contact support

features.name: Progress Tracking
features.description: Provide mastery map and trend data so students can see per-topic mastery, strength/weakness analysis, and score trends over time.
user_flows: GET /api/progress/mastery with Authorization -> Receive 200 with topic mastery map (subtopics with mastery scores and color codes) -> GET /api/progress/trends with Authorization -> Receive 200 with weekly/monthly trend series
GET /api/progress/trends with Authorization -> Receive 500 if DB or aggregation job fails -> Receive 500 'trend generation error' -> Client shows fallback message and retry option

features.name: Recommendations
features.description: AI-driven remedial and enrichment recommendations: a GET route for personalized next-up items and a POST route to generate targeted remedial assignments for weak topics.
user_flows: GET /api/recommendations with Authorization -> Receive 200 with list of recommended topics/actions (e.g., practice, watch video, remedial test) -> User taps remedial CTA -> POST /api/recommendations/remedial with Authorization and body {topicIds, targetDifficulty} -> Receive 201 with {remedialAssignmentId} -> GET /api/assignments/[remedialAssignmentId] with Authorization -> Receive 200 remedial assignment details
POST /api/recommendations/remedial with Authorization but missing or empty topicIds -> Receive 400 validation error -> Client displays validation error and prevents remedial generation
GET /api/recommendations with Authorization -> Receive 200 but empty recommendations (no data) -> Client shows 'no recommendations' fallback and CTA to take a diagnostic
POST /api/recommendations/remedial with Authorization -> Receive 429 Rate Limit (rate-limit from recommendation-engine or Gemini) -> Client surfaces rate-limit message and allowed retry-after window

features.name: Curriculum
features.description: Expose the full curriculum tree (subjects → chapters → topics → subtopics) and study-material metadata so the UI can build the curriculum browser and material cards. This endpoint is public (no auth required) for browsing.
user_flows: GET /api/curriculum without Authorization -> Receive 200 with curriculum tree including subjects, chapters, topics, and metadata (material counts, IDs) -> Client renders curriculum browser
GET /api/curriculum with query for subjectId (if supported) without Authorization -> Receive 200 with filtered chapters for subjectId
GET /api/curriculum -> Receive 500 DB error -> Receive 500 'curriculum fetch error' -> Client shows error state and retry option

features.name: Assignment Management
features.description: APIs for creating, listing, and generating AI assignments.

endpoints.description: List all assignments for the user

features.name: Submission and Evaluation
features.description: APIs for submitting assignment answers and retrieving evaluation.

features.name: Progress Tracking
features.description: APIs for user progress, mastery levels, and trends.

endpoints.description: Generate remedial assignment for weak topics
